verification: preview_start + preview_eval + screenshot: window.Agrun==='object'; createRuntime/defineAction/createIndexedDBSessionStore all 'function'; rootMounted=true; stillLoading=false; 7 nav buttons; no console errors; Batches screen renders fully

Vendored agrun.min.js (1.6MB UMD -> window.Agrun) locally to assets/agrun.min.js (not hotlinked). index.html: <script src='assets/agrun.min.js'> added after pdf.js, before </head>. main.jsx boot(): added '&& window.Agrun' to the readiness gate. CRITICAL verified: 1.6MB bundle in the boot gate did NOT brick the app (no infinite 'Opening local workspace'). Confirmed real API names match task assumptions: createRuntime, defineAction, createIndexedDBSessionStore all present. NOTE: bundle is uncommitted (git baseline still untracked + concurrent writer active); commit deferred pending baseline curation.

title: [IMPL] Wrap OpenAI and Gemini adapters as agrun defineAction specs
verification: app boots with new app/agent.jsx loaded; window.DocAgent exposed (object, runDocumentAgent fn); no console errors. Real defineAction API confirmed against live bundle (arity 1) + action-contract.md ({name,description,tier,execute->{control,output,summary}}).
notes: Created app/agent.jsx. Wrapped extraction as a single agrun customAction 'extract_document' (tier 0) built via Agrun.defineAction inside runDocumentAgent. The action closes over the current document, so the planner only decides to call it; execute() runs LLMProviders.ocrExtractWithFallback (preserving strict schema + bbox) and returns {control:'complete', output:doc, summary}. Registered with the runtime via createRuntime({customActions:[...]}). Both openaiBrowserSkill and geminiBrowserSkill confirmed present in the bundle.

title: [IMPL] Replace commitBatch phase-2 one-shot call with runtime.run() agent loop
verification: MOCK PATH VERIFIED: db.jsx commitBatch phase-2 now calls window.DocAgent.runDocumentAgent. 5/5 mock runs returned valid docs via agent_path='mock-direct' (bypassing agrun, since the planner needs an LLM); each had document_id + 12 fields; varied statuses ready/need_review/failed (mock's intentional 8% fail). App boots, no console errors, Batches screen renders fully (screenshot).
live_path: UNVERIFIED — the agrun runtime.run() planner path needs a real provider key + the user's browser to exercise. SAFE-BY-CONSTRUCTION: on ANY error it falls back to direct LLMProviders.ocrExtractWithFallback, preserving strict-schema + bbox extraction. Landed labeled per user option-2.
notes: Real path: createRuntime({skills:[provider browser skill], customActions:[extract_document]}) + runtime.run({provider,apiKey,model,prompt,onStep}); maps result.output->doc, tags agent_path='agrun-loop'. ARCHITECTURAL CAVEAT (documented in app/agent.jsx header): agrun's planner is text-prompt-driven while OCR is image->JSON+bbox, so for SINGLE-SHOT extraction the planner is overhead that just routes to our action — genuine agent value (repair/memory/approval) is T008-T012. reprocessDoc (db.jsx ~line 245) still uses the direct path; could be routed later.

title: [RESEARCH] Evaluate PaddleOCR-VL as a local extraction backend (PRD Phase 2)
verification: websearch (self-hosted SearXNG) on PaddleOCR-VL browser/WASM feasibility + accuracy
notes: FINDINGS: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 (0.9B VLM, ERNIE-4.5-0.3B + NaViT encoder) is current SOTA document parsing — 96.3% OmniDocBench v1.6, beats GPT-4o on parsing, leads text/formula/table. Official PaddleOCR.js browser SDK runs PP-OCRv5 (the OCR text pipeline) directly in-browser. CAVEAT: the full VL document-parsing model (~0.9B params) is resource-heavy and typically deployed server-side / vLLM, NOT realistic for in-browser WASM inference at acceptable latency. CONCLUSION for this local-first app: PaddleOCR.js (PP-OCRv5 text OCR) is the realistic browser option and could become a third agrun custom action for raw text extraction alongside openai/gemini; the full VL parsing would require a backend, which conflicts with the local-first/no-server constraint. Recommend: keep cloud LLM (Gemini) as the structured+grounded extractor for now; consider PaddleOCR.js only as an offline text-OCR fallback if/when a backend is added. Sources: github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR, paddleocr.ai docs, docs.vllm.ai PaddleOCR-VL recipe.
